entry.title: Stealth addresses using nostr
entry.summary:
In the realm of cryptocurrency and blockchain technologies, there are ongoing discussions and developments that capture the interest of enthusiasts and experts alike. One such topic is the innovative use of Nostr for notifications related to silent payments. This subject was broached in a thread on DelvingBitcoin.org, specifically at [this link](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/silent-payments-notifications-via-nostr/2203), indicating a burgeoning interest in leveraging Nostr for enhancing transactional notifications without directly engaging traditional, potentially less secure methods.
Further interest in this application of Nostr is evident through discussions and mentions on platforms like Xcancel.com, where individuals within the crypto community, including noted figures such as Francis Pouliot, are exploring its potential. For those interested in following these developments or diving deeper into the conversation, additional insights and updates can be found at [this source](https://xcancel.com/francispouliot_/status/2019219267322015931).
To ensure the availability of this discussion and its contents for posterity and wider access, an archive link has been created and can be visited here: [Archive.is](https://archive.is/v2oHW). This effort underscores the importance of the topic and the community's commitment to preserving knowledge and facilitating ongoing dialogue around the innovative uses of technology in the financial sector, particularly within the context of cryptocurrencies and blockchain.
